title: phegopteris phegopteris var. intermedia
intro: Phegopteris phegopteris var. intermedia, also known as the intermediate shield fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Phegopteris genus and is native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in the plant. The health benefits of Phegopteris phegopteris var. intermedia include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of certain diseases such as cancer and diabetes. In herbal preparations, Phegopteris phegopteris var. intermedia is typically dried and used as an extract or tea, or combined with other herbs to create a tonic or decoction.
